top_module.name: sram_mbist_demo_controller
top_module.description: Top-level controller providing register-mapped scratchpad access, BIST control/status, and interrupt generation while instantiating the explicit SRAM wrapper as child instance u_sram.
top_module.functionality: Implements a minimal MMIO-like register block and SRAM scratchpad controller. Software can program MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, and MEM_CONTROL to write or read the SRAM. MEM_RDATA returns the most recently captured SRAM read data. BIST control is exposed through both the bist_start input and the BIST_CONTROL register START bit, with status reflected in BIST_STATUS and IRQ_STATUS. The controller must instantiate demo_sram_32x256_wrapper as child instance u_sram, preserving the macro intent for downstream MBIST collateral generation.
responsibilities: Accept synchronous register-style write/read transactions on wr_en/wr_addr/wr_data and rd_en/rd_addr.
Decode CONTROL, STATUS, MEM_ADDR, MEM_WDATA, MEM_RDATA, MEM_CONTROL, BIST_CONTROL, BIST_STATUS, IRQ_STATUS, and IRQ_CLEAR registers.
Drive the child SRAM wrapper control signals csb, web, addr, and din, and consume dout.
Latch and present SRAM read data to MEM_RDATA and rd_data for register reads.
Track ready/busy semantics and expose BIST done/fail status.
Assert irq only when IRQ_ENABLE is set and either bist_done or bist_fail is latched in IRQ_STATUS.
Provide a simple BIST request path through bist_start and register bit START.
Keep reset behavior deterministic for downstream DFT and simulation.
Expose explicit memory intent for MBIST-aware tools through the child wrapper instance u_sram.
top_module.reset_behavior: On reset_n deassertion low, clear CONTROL ENABLE, SOFT_RESET, and IRQ_ENABLE; clear STATUS ready/busy indication; clear BIST done/fail/running state and IRQ_STATUS; clear last_fail_addr; clear software-visible read data; and place the SRAM interface into an inactive, deselected state until the controller is enabled and reset is released.
behavior_rules: CONTROL bit 0 gates normal enable behavior; CONTROL bit 1 requests a soft reset of controller-visible state; CONTROL bit 2 enables IRQ generation from latched BIST status.
STATUS reports ready, bist_done, bist_fail, and busy with ready asserted only when the controller is enabled and not busy.
MEM_ADDR selects the SRAM word address used by MEM_CONTROL write/read operations.
MEM_WDATA captures write data for SRAM writes.
MEM_RDATA is read-only and returns the most recently captured SRAM read data word.
MEM_CONTROL bit 0 causes a single SRAM write cycle using MEM_ADDR and MEM_WDATA; bit 1 causes a single SRAM read cycle using MEM_ADDR.
BIST_CONTROL bit 0 starts BIST, and bit 1 clears latched BIST results and IRQ_STATUS.
BIST_STATUS reflects done, fail, running, and last_fail_addr[7:0].
IRQ_STATUS latches bist_done and bist_fail events until cleared by IRQ_CLEAR or BIST_CONTROL.CLEAR_RESULT.
irq shall assert when IRQ_ENABLE is set and at least one IRQ_STATUS event is latched.
The controller shall use the child wrapper instance named u_sram exactly as the visible memory child for downstream macro recognition.
The top module is not a raw SRAM model; memory implementation resides in the child wrapper/model path.
The design must remain synthesizable without vendor-only primitives in the fallback path.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x256_wrapper
modules.description: Functional scratchpad wrapper for the 256x32 SRAM, with a fixed interface suitable for binding to the prebuilt Sky130 macro sky130_sram_1kbyte_1rw1r_32x256_8 when available or to the synthesizable fallback model when collateral is unavailable. The wrapper is instantiated by the top-level controller as child instance u_sram.
modules.functionality: Provides the canonical SRAM access wrapper interface used by the top-level controller. When the prebuilt Sky130 SRAM collateral is present, this wrapper binds to that macro; otherwise it binds to the synthesizable demo_sram_32x256_model. The wrapper must preserve the exact clk/csb/web/addr/din/dout contract so MBIST tools can identify or replace the memory path without changing the controller interface.
responsibilities: Expose a stable single-port functional memory interface with active-low chip select and write enable semantics.
Bind to the prebuilt SRAM macro when available, otherwise bind to the fallback model.
Keep the macro intent explicit for synthesis, DFT, and MBIST collateral extraction.
Avoid changing the top-level controller ports or register behavior.
If an underlying macro exposes additional read ports, leave them unused or reserved for future MBIST wrapper integration.
Maintain synthesis-friendly behavior with no vendor-specific primitives in the fallback path.
modules.reset_behavior: No explicit reset port is present. On system reset via the parent controller, the wrapper shall be held deselected through csb/web control and may return unknown/implementation-defined contents until written or initialized by the model path.
behavior_rules: csb is active-low chip enable; when deasserted, memory access is disabled.
web is active-low write enable for the functional access path.
addr selects one of 256 32-bit words.
din provides write data and dout provides read data.
The wrapper must be structurally recognizable as the memory access point for downstream macro/MBIST tooling.
The wrapper instance name in the top-level controller is u_sram.
The wrapper may be implemented by generate-time binding to either the prebuilt macro or the fallback model, but the interface shall remain identical.

modules.name: demo_sram_32x256_model
modules.description: Synthesizable fallback memory model for the 256x32 scratchpad when the prebuilt Sky130 SRAM collateral is not available during RTL generation. This module keeps the same functional wrapper interface as demo_sram_32x256_wrapper.
modules.functionality: Implements a simple synthesizable word-addressed memory model matching the wrapper interface. Writes occur on the active clock edge when csb is low and web is low; reads are observed on the functional access path when csb is low and web is high. The model is intended only as a fallback for simulation and synthesis continuity and must not be treated as a replacement for the prebuilt macro intent.
responsibilities: Provide a synthesizable memory fallback with the same interface as the wrapper.
Preserve the 256x32 storage shape.
Support straightforward replacement by the prebuilt SRAM macro or MBIST wrapper.
Avoid any vendor-specific memory primitive dependencies.
Keep timing and functional behavior simple for fast regression runs.
modules.reset_behavior: No explicit reset port is present. Memory contents may be unknown after reset unless initialized by a testbench; the parent controller must not rely on cleared storage contents.
behavior_rules: Use clk as the only clock.
Treat csb low as selected.
Treat web low as write, web high as read.
Use addr as an 8-bit word address for 32-bit data.
Do not infer vendor-only or technology-specific RAM primitives.
The fallback model exists solely to preserve interface compatibility for synthesis and simulation when macro collateral is unavailable.

top_level_connections.description: Top-level port clk connected to matching child module port(s).

inter_module_signals.description: Derived internal top-owned signal driving demo_sram_32x256_wrapper.addr.

inter_module_signals.description: Derived internal top-owned signal driving demo_sram_32x256_wrapper.din.
